task_completion.visual_evidence: The QBlade graphical user interface is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Load the NREL 5MW rotor project from the sample projects directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The QBlade interface shows the NREL 5MW project loaded, typically visible in the project tree, module list, or 3D view.

task_completion.description: Configure a steady-state BEM simulation with a Tip Speed Ratio (TSR) sweep.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A simulation setup or definition window is open in QBlade, showing parameters for a BEM simulation and a range defined for a TSR (Tip Speed Ratio or Lambda) sweep.

task_completion.description: Execute the BEM simulation to generate Cp vs TSR data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph or results table is visible in QBlade displaying the simulation results, specifically showing Cp (Power Coefficient) plotted against TSR (Tip Speed Ratio).

task_completion.description: Export the Cp vs TSR data to /home/ga/Documents/projects/bem_results.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export/save dialog box showing the filename 'bem_results.txt' and the path '/home/ga/Documents/projects/', or a terminal/file manager confirming the file exists at this exact location.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the QBlade GUI to perform the simulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through QBlade menus, buttons, and dialogs rather than just running a script in the terminal.

integrity.description: The exported data comes from an actual simulation run in QBlade.
integrity.visual_evidence: The QBlade interface displays the generated Cp vs TSR graph before exporting, proving the data wasn't manually typed or copied from an external source.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually create the results file using a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no screenshots of the agent typing simulation data into a text editor (like nano, vim, or gedit) to fake the bem_results.txt file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses QBlade v0.96.2. The NREL 5MW Reference Wind Turbine has a documented maximum power coefficient (Cp) of approximately 0.482-0.486 at an optimal TSR of 7.55. The expected output is a multi-column numeric text file containing Cp and TSR columns, exported exactly to /home/ga/Documents/projects/bem_results.txt.
